title: achillea pindicola subsp. integrifolia
intro: Achillea pindicola subsp. integrifolia, also known as mountain yarrow or Greek mountain yarrow, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for wound healing and treating digestive issues. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting digestive health. Achillea pindicola subsp. integrifolia can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
